title: Subitem Budget Totals Reflected in Parent Budget Column
excerpt: I keep trying to find an answer to this that does not involve a third party app that I have to pay more money for or using “Show Summary in Parent Item” so I’m posting again in hops of being directed to the right place o…

posts.author: sadamo20 (stephanie.adamo@thejamesmuseum.org)
posts.cookedHtml:
I keep trying to find an answer to this that does not involve a third party app that I have to pay more money for or using “Show Summary in Parent Item” so I’m posting again in hops of being directed to the right place or beg for this to be changed by Monday.com.

Can Monday.com please automatically have budget numbers on a subitem be included in the parent budget coloumn? Or can anyone show me how to do this?

The “Show Summary in Parent Item” does not get reflected in Parent budget column so more items are created and I had to create a dashboard to see the total budget for a project. This seems like a basic function in any other project managment software or spreadsheet I’ve used.

I also don’t want to have to pay to use a third party app. As a non-profit, every dollar counts and we can work around this, but it’s annoying and we don’t want to have to create dashboards for everything.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Stephanie,

Sorry to hear of your frustration!

I want to make sure I am understanding exactly what you’re trying to achieve here. Are you looking to combine the subitem total into the parent item total? You’re welcome to share/annotate a screenshot so I can take this with the relevant team internally!

posts.author: mark.valois (Mark Valois)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Bianca,
I have the same question. When creating a “Show Summary on Parent Item” there is a new column created with the total of the subitem listed in that newly created column.

However, I already have a column in the parent item list called ‘Budget’.

Is there a way to have the sub-item total go to the parent item column?

Thank you.

posts.author: i-jaglan (Sagar Jaglan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @sadamo20 @mark.valois,

Totally understand the frustration — this really should be native functionality. The “Show Summary on Parent Item” creates a
mirror-type column that doesn’t write back to your actual Budget column, which makes it pretty useless for dashboards, formulas,
or reporting.

Unfortunately monday.com still hasn’t added this natively, but SubItems Pro can solve this in about 30 seconds:

  1. Add SubItems Pro as a Board View
  2. Create a Rollup rule: Subitem Budget → SUM → Parent Budget column
  3. Enable Auto-Sync and save the changes

It writes to your existing numbers column — no extra mirror columns, no dashboards needed as workarounds. When any subitem budget
changes, the parent total updates automatically.

Regarding cost — I hear you, especially as a non-profit. SubItems Pro has a free tier (1 board, 1 rule) which might be all you need if your budget tracking lives on a single board. Unlimited syncs within that board, no per-action charges for rollups. If you need more boards, paid plans start at $8/mo.

You can try it out here: SubItems Pro on the Marketplace

Happy to help with setup if you have questions!
